commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And when David heard of it, he sent Joab, and all the army of the mighty men. The outrage offered to the ambassadors was a sufficient reason. The king of Ammon might have refused to receive them; but he could not, with any propriety, treat them with scorn. "The right of ambassadors has both a divine and human sanction. "(Cicero, contra Verrem 3. ) The Romans have frequently waged war to revenge such wrongs. (Grotius, Jur.
